messages.content:
You are a technical writing assistant. You rephrase Python function docstrings while preserving their exact semantics.
Rules:
- Keep the function signature (name, parameters, return type annotation) exactly unchanged.
- Keep any import statements exactly unchanged.
- Keep doctest examples (lines starting with >>> or ...) exactly unchanged.
- Rephrase only the natural language description inside the docstring.
- Make each variant meaningfully different from the others.
- Return a JSON array of 4 strings, each containing one complete paraphrased function. No markdown fences, no other text.

def fix_spaces(text):
    """
    Given a string text, replace all spaces in it with underscores, 
    and if a string has more than 2 consecutive spaces, 
    then replace all consecutive spaces with - 
    
    fix_spaces("Example") == "Example"
    fix_spaces("Example 1") == "Example_1"
    fix_spaces(" Example 2") == "_Example_2"
    fix_spaces(" Example   3") == "_Example-3"
    """

samples.content:
["def fix_spaces(text):
    """
    For any input string, change all space characters to underscores. However, if there are three or more consecutive spaces, convert the entire sequence of spaces to a single dash.
    fix_spaces("Example") == "Example"
    fix_spaces("Example 1") == "Example_1"
    fix_spaces(" Example 2") == "_Example_2"
    fix_spaces(" Example   3") == "_Example-3"
    """", "def fix_spaces(text):
    """
    This function takes a text string and replaces all spaces with underscores. If more than two consecutive spaces are present, they are replaced with a hyphen.
    fix_spaces("Example") == "Example"
    fix_spaces("Example 1") == "Example_1"
    fix_spaces(" Example 2") == "_Example_2"
    fix_spaces(" Example   3") == "_Example-3"
    """", "def fix_spaces(text):
    """
    Convert spaces to underscores in the given text, except when there are multiple consecutive spaces exceeding two, in which case use a dash.
    fix_spaces("Example") == "Example"
    fix_spaces("Example 1") == "Example_1"
    fix_spaces(" Example 2") == "_Example_2"
    fix_spaces(" Example   3") == "_Example-3"
    """", "def fix_spaces(text):
    """
    The process involves substituting underscores for spaces. In instances where three or more spaces appear consecutively, the entire group is replaced with a dash.
    fix_spaces("Example") == "Example"
    fix_spaces("Example 1") == "Example_1"
    fix_spaces(" Example 2") == "_Example_2"
    fix_spaces(" Example   3") == "_Example-3"
    """"]
